intended_use: Jazz is intended for the labeling, visualization and volumetric quantification of segmentable brain structures from a set of MR images, for patients with a known diagnosis of multiple sclerosis (for t
intended_use_full: Jazz is intended for the labeling, visualization and volumetric quantification of segmentable brain structures from a set of MR images, for patients with a known diagnosis of multiple sclerosis (for the multiple sclerosis pipeline) and/or brain metastasis (for the metastasis pipeline), and the production of a radiological report.
ground_truth: gold standard human expert opinion for segmentation; voxel-wise sensitivity, specificity, and dice score metrics; anatomy localization scoring; coregistration quality scoring
other_metrics: lesion-wise dice score of at least 0.5 for multiple sclerosis models; lesion-wise true positive rate of at least 60% and false negative rate of at most 40% for high sensitivity model; lesion-wise false discovery rate of at most 50% for high specificity model; anatomy localization score of 1 (best) in at least 80% of cases; coregistration score average larger than 4 (good); reproducibility tested with identical lesion counts and volumes in process-retest experiments
